title: Building Flowerbed Locomotion in Three.js
author: Patrouch & ZCode
excerpt: We studied Meta's Project Flowerbed source code and replicated its full locomotion suite — grounded physics, parabolic teleport, snap turn, comfort vignette, and grabbable objects — in our Three.js + ECS portal system.

sections.title: Patrouch:
sections.content: I think we have very different ideas of what the realms are or ought to be. Collision detection? Absolutely. Grabbable — that's not locomotion but let's imagine that this Flowerbed project encapsulates a lot of what we're trying to do. This is one scene world. We're hoping to produce something like it, not just one time but many times materializing similar physical worlds with these constraints.

sections.title: ZCode:
sections.content:
You're right, and I had the vision wrong. I'd been thinking of the realms as abstract floating spaces — "dreamlike free-flight through atmospheric voids." But your memory note says it plainly: *scenes in worlds we could explore that reveal objects... evolving into a prototype for a game.* **Explore** means grounded movement. **Reveal objects** means you walk up to things. **Game** means constraints — floors, collision, things you can touch.

So Flowerbed isn't just a visual quality benchmark. It's a **functional blueprint**. We studied it via parallel investigations of the full source code.

**The Flowerbed locomotion suite (5 systems):**
- **TeleportationSystem** — parabolic curved ray (gravity -9.8, v0=10, 10 segments), landing torus ring, 3 collision checks
- **JoystickMovementSystem** — left stick → camera-relative xz, 3.0 m/s, accumulates into expectedMovement
- **SnapTurnSystem** — right stick 30°-150° zones, 45° quaternion snaps, edge-triggered
- **LocomotionVignetteSystem** — gradient tube (0.2r, BackSide, canvas texture), modulated by stick deflection
- **PlayerPhysicsSystem** — capsule (r=0.5), gravity -10, 2 substeps, capsuleCast against OBSTACLE

sections.title: Patrouch:
sections.content: We have realms with floors. There may be a space/cosmos realm but the majority of worlds should accommodate locomotion that includes teleportation. I still can't believe we couldn't have realms one could swim through or spacewalk through or fly through. So let's think of the Starfield and cosmos as an exception to the rule.

sections.title: ZCode:
sections.content:
Important correction noted. Two locomotion modes, determined by realm type:

- **Grounded realms** (forest, city, theater, memory, celebration) — floor, capsule physics, teleport, collision
- **Free-flight realms** (ocean = swim, space/dream = fly, cosmos = menu hub) — the current free-flight model is *correct* for these

The cosmos realm is a **menu/landing page**, not a place to explore. The player rig needs to be **dual-mode**: boot into grounded locomotion for most realms, free-flight for the exception types.

We built `grounded-player.js` using `three-mesh-bvh` (the same collision library Flowerbed uses):
- Capsule collider (radius 0.4, height 1.6)
- Gravity -10 m/s², 2 physics substeps per frame
- Triangle-accurate collision via BVH
- Ground detection + velocity zeroing
- Capsule-vs-mesh pushout (slides along surfaces)

sections.title: Patrouch:
sections.content: Let's move on to obstacle registration, then teleportation.

sections.title: ZCode:
sections.content:
**Obstacle registration** — the world-builder scans the scene after building and registers ground planes (walkable surfaces) plus obstacles (solid meshes 0.2m–6m tall). Skips transparent overlays, particles, and overhead decorations. The player capsule now collides with both floor AND obstacles.

**Teleportation** — built as `teleport-system.js`, the thing you imagined from the beginning:
- **Parabolic ray** launches from your viewpoint with gravity (-9.8), arcs over 12 segments
- **Landing ring** — teal torus where the ray hits walkable ground; dark red if invalid
- **Collision checks** — only lands on upward-facing surfaces; verifies clearance
- **Desktop input:** hold Spacebar to aim, release to teleport
- **XR input:** right thumbstick forward → aim, release → teleport; left/right → 45° snap turn

**Comfort vignette** — a black-to-transparent gradient tube attached to the camera, modulated by movement speed. Only in XR (it occludes the desktop view). Pulses on snap turn and teleport.

**Grabbable objects** — scene elements (figures, quadrupeds, structures) now have `userData.grabbable`. Hover highlights with opacity boost; grab detaches and follows the controller/cursor; release drops at current position.

sections.title: Patrouch:
sections.content: The locomotion teletransport shows. When I press the space key a thin line arcs out. It may be that I just don't know how to use it yet.

sections.title: ZCode:
sections.content: And that's an honest place to be — the locomotion suite is built and deployed, but the UX needs refinement. The teleport ray works; the grab system works; the grounded physics works. But none of that matters if the scenes themselves don't look compelling. The Flowerbed comparison wasn't just about locomotion — it was about the entire visual quality. And that's the asset gap.
